title: Gaussian Plume Atmospheric Dispersion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Gaussian Plume Atmospheric Dispersion: Gaussian plume: infer point source emission rate Q and location from downwind concentration measurements. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.gaussian.plume_model…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~25; wind_direction_uncertainty_deg, stability_class_error set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.world_state_x: source emission rate Q (g/s), source height H (m), source location (x0, y0)
E.observation_y: ambient concentration measurements C_i (ug/m3) at N_sensors downwind

W.regime: Existence of the recovered source_strength_location is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 25); wind_direction_uncertainty_deg dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: Q estimate within 30% for stable conditions. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~25. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |wind_direction_uncertainty_deg|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_sensors * N_sources) per evaluation per iteration
